comments: Clear mechanistic insight from structure-function correlation, though the slow-blocking kinetics warrant detailed kinetic modeling to quantify on/off rates and clinical relevance.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Menthol inhibits TRPV5 through interaction with W583
hypotheses.result: Confirmed by cryo-EM structure showing direct menthol-W583 contact

title: Proton channels govern vesicular carbonate chemistry in mineralizing cells of a marine calcifier
task: Elucidate proton channel regulation of pH and carbonate chemistry in calcifying vesicles during biomineralization
key_results: Otop2l proton channel regulates intravesicular alkaline pH in ACC-forming endocytic vesicles; channel activated by alkaline pH, Mg²⁺, and Ca²⁺; high proton conductance maintains mineralization-favorable conditions
comments: Mechanistically rich study linking ion channels to biomineralization, though the interplay between membrane potential driving proton flux and vesicle exocytosis kinetics could be quantified more thoroughly.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Otop2l controls alkaline pH in calcifying vesicles via membrane potential-driven proton transport
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; Otop2l is activated by alkaline conditions and divalent cations

hypotheses.hypothesis: High intravesicular pH facilitates ACC precursor formation
hypotheses.result: Inferred from alkaline pH recordings in mineralizing vesicles

title: Magnetic resonance control of spin-correlated radical pair dynamics in vivo
task: Demonstrate magnetic resonance control of spin-correlated radical pair dynamics in living organisms
key_results: RF magnetic fields near electron spin resonance frequency modulate red fluorescent protein emission in C. elegans expressing mScarlet; coherence time >4 ns suggests quantum-correlated radical pair mechanism
comments: Groundbreaking in vivo demonstration of quantum-biological control, but the functional relevance and potential off-target effects of RF fields on endogenous cellular processes require careful evaluation.

inputs: static + RF magnetic fields near ESR frequency

hypotheses.hypothesis: RFP-flavin systems exhibit magnetic-field-sensitive radical pair reactions in vivo
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; RF fields modulate fluorescence in transgenic C. elegans

title: In vivo site-specific engineering to reprogram T cells
task: In vivo site-specific integration of CAR transgene into T cells without ex vivo manufacturing
key_results: Therapeutic levels of CAR T cells generated in vivo in humanized mouse models of B cell aplasia, hematological and solid malignancies; stable, cell-specific transgene expression achieved through site-specific integration
comments: Eliminates costly ex vivo manufacturing bottleneck, though translation to human clinical efficacy and durability post-integration remain to be demonstrated.

model_io.model: Two-vector CRISPR-based in vivo T cell engineering system
inputs: DNA donor template with CAR transgene (adeno-associated virus)
outputs: Site-specific CAR transgene integration at T cell locus
Therapeutic CAR T cell populations in vivo

hypotheses.hypothesis: T cell-optimized two-vector delivery enables site-specific CAR integration with durability superior to transient expression
hypotheses.result: Stable, therapeutic CAR T cell generation demonstrated in vivo

hypotheses.hypothesis: Site-specific integration at endogenous locus prevents genotoxicity of random integration
hypotheses.result: Safe integration achieved without mention of adverse genotoxic events

title: Caspase-8 comes in from the CYLD
task: Elucidate role of Caspase-8-mediated CYLD cleavage in Caspase-11 expression and endotoxicity
key_results: Caspase-8 cleavage of CYLD required for Caspase-11 expression in LPS-induced endotoxicity; provides mechanism linking Caspase-8 to Caspase-11-dependent cell death pathway
comments: Commentary highlighting mechanistic insight; original experimental data not provided in abstract.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Caspase-8-mediated CYLD cleavage is required for Caspase-11 expression and endotoxic response
hypotheses.result: Supported: identified as missing link in Caspase-8 endotoxicity mechanism

title: Repression of EGFR by new biguanide 4C potentiated ovarian cancer to PARP inhibitors through down-regulation of BRCA2 and Rad51
task: Determine how EGFR repression by biguanide 4C sensitizes BRCA1/2 WT ovarian cancer to PARP inhibitors
key_results: 4C downregulated EGFR, reducing nuclear BRCA2/Rad51 accumulation; enhanced BRCA2/Rad51 binding to c-Cbl E3 ligase; increased BRCA2/Rad51 ubiquitination and degradation; synergistic 4C+PARPi inhibited HR and increased DNA damage sensitivity in BRCA1/2 WT cells
comments: Reveals non-canonical EGFR function in HR regulation but mechanism of ATM-mediated nuclear-cytoplasmic signaling requires structural clarity.

model_io.model: BRCA1/2 WT ovarian cancer cell lines

hypotheses.hypothesis: EGFR promotes HR by blocking BRCA2/Rad51-c-Cbl interaction
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: EGFR downregulation enhances c-Cbl binding and ubiquitin-mediated degradation

hypotheses.hypothesis: 4C+PARPi combination achieves synthetic lethality in BRCA1/2 WT OC
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: synergistic inhibition of HR and increased sensitivity to DNA damage

title: Mitochondrial DNA release via VDAC1 in keratinocytes: a key driver of innate immunity and vitiligo pathogenesis
task: Elucidate mitochondrial DNA release via VDAC1 in keratinocyte innate immunity and vitiligo pathogenesis
key_results: H₂O₂ induced VDAC1 oligomerization and selective mtDNA release under non-apoptotic conditions; mtDNA activated cGAS-STING and NLRP3 inflammasome pathways; VDAC1 inhibition (genetic silencing or VBIT-4) blocked mtDNA release, suppressed inflammatory cascades, alleviated depigmentation and CD8⁺ T cell infiltration in vitiligo mice
comments: Identifies VDAC1-dependent mtDNA release as pivotal link between oxidative stress and autoimmunity; mechanism of VDAC1 oligomerization coordination with mPTP requires structural characterization.

model_io.model: Murine vitiligo model (oxidative stress-induced or genetic)

hypotheses.hypothesis: H₂O₂ induces VDAC1 oligomerization enabling selective mtDNA release under non-apoptotic conditions
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: VDAC1 inhibition blocks mtDNA release and downstream signaling

hypotheses.hypothesis: mtDNA release via VDAC1 drives cGAS-STING and NLRP3 inflammasome activation in keratinocytes
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: mtDNA acts as danger signal; VDAC1 inhibition suppresses both pathways

title: Impact of C4BPA on Muscle progenitor cell differentiation: insights for Duchenne muscular dystrophy treatment
task: Determine C4BPA's role as anti-myogenic factor in Duchenne muscular dystrophy FAPs
key_results: DMD FAPs secrete elevated C4BPA (⇧368/760 detected proteins); C4BPA impaired myotube formation (↓myotube area, ↓nuclei/myotube); downregulated myogenic markers, upregulated atrophy genes; reduced contractile function in 3D engineered muscle; C4BPA silencing in DMD cultures partially restored myogenic capacity
comments: Identifies novel FAP-derived soluble inhibitor of myogenesis; mechanism of C4BPA signaling on myoblasts requires receptor identification.

model_io.model: Co-culture myoblasts with FAPs (healthy vs DMD-derived)

hypotheses.hypothesis: DMD FAPs secrete elevated C4BPA inhibiting myogenic differentiation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: C4BPA suppresses myotube formation and myogenic markers; silencing restores capacity

title: DUSP12 promotes cell cycle progression and protects cells from ZNF622 mediated apoptosis
task: Determine DUSP12's role in cell cycle progression and protection from ZNF622-mediated apoptosis
key_results: DUSP12 interacts with zinc finger protein ZNF622 via zinc-binding domain; DUSP12 dephosphorylates ZNF622 at Ser143; DUSP12 overexpression promoted ZNF622 dephosphorylation, protected from stress-induced apoptosis; DUSP12 knockdown caused pre-metaphase and metaphase mitotic defects; ZNF622 overexpression increased pre-metaphase mitotic defects
comments: Identifies novel DUSP12-ZNF622 interaction regulating cell cycle and apoptosis but substrate specificity of DUSP12 and cell cycle mechanism details incomplete.

hypotheses.hypothesis: DUSP12 protects cells from ZNF622-mediated apoptosis via dephosphorylation at Ser143
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: DUSP12 knockdown promotes apoptosis; ZNF622 phosphomimetic/phospho-deficient mutants inactive

hypotheses.hypothesis: DUSP12 regulates ZNF622 function in cell cycle progression
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: DUSP12 knockdown and ZNF622 overexpression both cause mitotic defects

title: Celcomen: spatial causal disentanglement for single-cell and tissue perturbation modeling
task: Disentangle intra- and inter-cellular gene regulation in spatial transcriptomics via causal graphs
key_results: Celcomen leverages graph neural networks to disentangle gene regulation programs in spatial transcriptomics; generates post-perturbation counterfactual transcriptomics in human glioblastoma, fetal spleen, mouse lung cancer; demonstrates disentanglement, identifiability of causal structure, counterfactual prediction capabilities
comments: Offers computational framework for virtual tissue perturbation modeling but validation against ground-truth causal networks limited to simulations.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Causal disentanglement enables prediction of post-perturbation gene expression changes in spatial context
hypotheses.result: Supported: validated on human glioblastoma, fetal spleen, mouse lung cancer samples

title: Intellectual disability-causing mutations in KIF11 impair microtubule dynamics and dendritic arborization
task: Determine KIF11's role in microtubule dynamics and dendritic development in neurons
key_results: KIF11 depletion increases minus-end-out MT dynamics in axons/dendrites; MCLID mutations (KIF11Y82F, KIF11ΔCterm) reduce MT dynamics and impair dendritic arborization; KIF11ΔCterm disrupts tetramer formation; KIF11Y82F reduces MT sliding velocity; photo-controllable KIF11 inhibition increases dendritic growth
comments: Establishes KIF11 as an MT dynamics rheostat with clear mechanistic links to dendritic morphology, though in vivo relevance to microcephaly pathogenesis requires validation.

hypotheses.hypothesis: KIF11 acts as an MT dynamics rheostat in postmitotic neurons
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: depletion increases MT dynamics; MCLID mutations decrease dynamics

hypotheses.hypothesis: Reduced MT dynamics impairs dendritic arborization and synaptic function
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: MCLID mutations reduce arborization and mEPSC frequency

title: Loss of polarity by Cdc42 depletion and oncogenic Kras activation in the mouse intestinal epithelia leads to a necrotizing enterocolitis (NEC)-like disease
task: Determine mechanism by which Cdc42 loss and oncogenic Kras activation cause NEC-like disease in intestinal epithelium
key_results: Combined Cdc42 depletion and oncogenic Kras expression in intestinal epithelium caused small intestine failure (weight loss, inflammation, necroptosis, lethality); phenotype characterized by loss of intestinal stem cells, disrupted architecture, altered Hippo signaling, elevated IL-1/TNFα, necroptotic cell death; suppression of YAP, IL-1/TNFα, or necroptosis rescued pathology
comments: Reveals polarity-YAP-inflammatory-necroptotic axis in NEC pathogenesis but mechanism of YAP dysregulation downstream of polarity loss requires clarification.

model_io.model: Mouse intestinal epithelium (Cdc42 KO + oncogenic Kras activation)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Combined polarity loss (Cdc42) and Kras activation activates YAP-IL1/TNFα-necroptosis axis
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: YAP, IL-1, TNFα, or necroptosis inhibition rescues intestinal pathology

title: FGFR signaling establishes spatial gradients of secretory cell identities along the airway proximal-distal axis
task: Determine FGFR2b-mediated patterning of secretory cell identities along airway proximal-distal axis
key_results: Single-cell analysis revealed opposing gene-expression gradients along airway axis: proximal (innate immunity genes) vs distal (lipid metabolism, antigen presentation); lineage tracing showed sequential gradient establishment postnatally; Fgfr2b regulated distal airway lipid biosynthesis/vesicle trafficking, down-regulated innate immunity; organoid experiments confirmed Fgfr2b control of airway patterning
comments: Establishes morphogenic gradient mechanism in lung development; underlying FGFR2b transcriptional targets and downstream effectors require identification.

outputs: innate immunity vs lipid metabolism scores

hypotheses.hypothesis: FGFR2b establishes spatial gradients of secretory cell identity along proximal-distal axis
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: Fgfr2b induces distal identity (lipid metabolism, antigen presentation); suppresses proximal identity (innate immunity)

title: Paraspeckle condensation is controlled via TDP-43 polymerization and linked to neuroprotection
task: Determine TDP-43's role in regulating paraspeckle condensation and neuroprotection
key_results: TDP-43 inhibits NEAT1_2 ribonucleoprotein condensation in concentration-dependent manner; deletion of 3′-end UG repeat increases paraspeckle stability and cytoprotection in stressed neurons; longer 3′-end UG repeats linked to shorter ALS survival
comments: The study elegantly connects biomolecular condensate dynamics to disease pathology but cellular mechanism of TDP-43 sequestration during stress requires further structural detail.

hypotheses.hypothesis: TDP-43 polymerization suppresses NEAT1_2 paraspeckle condensation via RNA binding
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: concentration-dependent inhibition requires intact polymerization and RNA binding

hypotheses.hypothesis: 3′-end UG repeats mediate TDP-43-dependent paraspeckle regulation post-assembly
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: UG repeat deletion increases paraspeckle stability and cytoprotection

title: Cardioprotective effects of curcumin and piperine against obesity induced oxidative stress in rats
task: Assess cardioprotective effects of curcumin and piperine against obesity-induced oxidative stress
key_results: High-fat diet (HFD) in rats increased lipid profiles (TC, TG), decreased antioxidants (CAT, GPx, SOD), increased oxidative stress (MDA, NO, ROS) and inflammation (TNF-α, IL-1, IL-6), reduced Na⁺/K⁺-ATPase activity; curcumin+piperine co-treatment suppressed cardiac oxidative stress, inflammation, and histological damage
comments: Demonstrates herbal combination antioxidant effect but mechanism of synergy and tissue-specific bioavailability of compounds unclear.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Curcumin+piperine combination suppresses HFD-induced cardiac oxidative stress and inflammation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: treatment restored antioxidant enzymes, reduced oxidative/inflammatory markers, improved cardiac histology

title: Nonlinear dynamics of seizure suppression via optogenetic modulation of neuron-astrocyte interaction
task: Model nonlinear optogenetic suppression of seizures via neuron-astrocyte interaction
key_results: Astrocytic optogenetic stimulation (ChR2) reduced seizure severity by enhancing Na⁺ influx and Na⁺/K⁺-ATPase activity; accelerated K⁺ clearance and reduced neuronal excitability; effect persisted when astrocytic Ca²⁺ signaling or Kir4.1 impaired; combined astrocyte stimulation and neuronal inhibition synergistic; pre-seizure activation more effective than post-onset
comments: Computational model reveals Na⁺/K⁺-ATPase as primary anticonvulsant mechanism; in vivo translation and off-target effects require experimental validation.

model_io.model: Three-compartment computational model (neurons, astrocytes, extracellular space)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Astrocytic optogenetic stimulation suppresses seizures via Na⁺/K⁺-ATPase-driven K⁺ clearance
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: Na⁺ influx activation primary anticonvulsant; effect independent of Ca²⁺ signaling/Kir4.1

hypotheses.hypothesis: Combined astrocyte stimulation and neuronal inhibition shows synergistic anticonvulsant effect
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: further decreased K⁺ and neuronal spiking

title: Modulation of resting macrophage activity via low-level laser therapy (LLLT) and α-lipoic acid: an in vitro study using a PCL-based biomaterial
task: Assess combined effects of low-level laser therapy and α-lipoic acid on macrophage function
key_results: LLLT (808 nm, 200 mW, 5 J/cm²) and α-lipoic acid (LA) on PCL biomaterial increased macrophage viability; modulated MMP-2/9 activity; reduced AK, NO, TNF-α, MCP-1/CCL2; increased antioxidant capacity; combined treatment showed synergistic immunomodulation
comments: Demonstrates biomaterial-based immunomodulation but lacks mechanistic detail on LLLT signaling and dose-response characterization.

hypotheses.hypothesis: LA-modified PCL and LLLT synergistically modulate resting macrophage to pro-regenerative phenotype
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: combined treatment increased viability, enhanced antioxidant capacity, reduced inflammatory mediators

title: Impact of the CYFIP2 R87C variant in a human neuronal model in vitro
task: Assess impact of CYFIP2 R87C variant on neuronal morphology and neurodevelopment
key_results: R87C NPCs showed reduced motility and altered lamellipodia; R87C cortical organoids at day 30 showed increased size, decreased CYFIP2 protein, absence of SOX2+ cells indicating premature progenitor depletion; 2D neuronal cultures showed normal markers and electrophysiology
comments: Highlights critical divergence between 2D and 3D models, with organoids revealing developmental defects missed in monolayers; mechanism of progenitor depletion remains unclear.

model_io.model: CRISPR-edited hPSC lines (CYFIP2 R87C and WT)

hypotheses.hypothesis: CYFIP2 R87C impairs cytoskeletal dynamics in NPCs affecting lamellipodia and migration
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: reduced NPC motility and altered morphology

hypotheses.hypothesis: R87C causes developmental defects in 3D but not 2D neuronal contexts
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: organoids show progenitor depletion and increased size; 2D cultures normal

title: Propionate metabolism is dysregulated in non-small cell lung cancer patients and EGFR-mutant drug-tolerant persister cells
task: Elucidate propionate metabolism dysregulation as non-genetic mechanism of EGFR-TKI resistance in NSCLC
key_results: Propionate metabolism dysregulated in NSCLC patients: elevated MMA in tumors and serum of metastatic disease; MMAB downregulated in NSCLC and drug-tolerant persister cells; MMA accumulation activates EMT; restoring MMAB suppressed TGFβ signaling and enhanced TKI response
comments: Identifies novel metabolic mechanism of TKI resistance but in vivo significance of MMAB restoration and therapeutic timeline require clinical validation.

model_io.model: NSCLC cell lines (EGFR-mutant, drug-tolerant persister cells)

hypotheses.hypothesis: MMAB downregulation causes MMA accumulation driving EMT and TKI resistance
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: restoring MMAB suppresses EMT and enhances TKI response

title: 5-HMF inhibits glucocorticoid-induced osteoporosis through the VEGFR2/PI3K/AKT pathway
task: Prevent glucocorticoid-induced osteoporosis via VEGFR2/PI3K/AKT pathway
key_results: 5-HMF enhanced VEGFR2 phosphorylation and activated PI3K/AKT signaling; increased osteogenic differentiation markers (ALP, ARS staining) in MC3T3-E1 and BMSCs; improved bone density in dexamethasone-treated mice
comments: The study demonstrates pathway activation but relies on pharmacological AKT inhibition rather than genetic validation of pathway necessity.

hypotheses.hypothesis: 5-HMF activates PI3K/AKT pathway to promote osteogenic differentiation and inhibit apoptosis
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: VEGFR2 phosphorylation, PI3K/AKT activation, increased osteogenic markers, reduced Dex-induced apoptosis

hypotheses.hypothesis: AKT activity is required for 5-HMF's protective effects
hypotheses.result: Supported: MK2206 (AKT inhibitor) abrogated 5-HMF benefits

title: PTP4A3 contributes to pathological retinal neovascularization and vascular leakage partly through the PI3K-AKT signalling pathway
task: Elucidate PTP4A3's role in diabetic retinopathy-related endothelial dysfunction via PI3K-AKT pathway
key_results: PTP4A3 elevated in human diabetic retinopathy and STZ/OIR models; overexpression increased EC proliferation/migration and disrupted barrier (↓ Occludin, Claudin-5); PTP4A3 inhibition reversed dysfunction in vitro and ameliorated retinal vascular leakage/neovascularization in vivo; PI3K/AKT inhibition partly alleviated dysfunction
comments: Identifies a druggable target but the substrate of PTP4A3 and complete mechanism of barrier disruption remain unspecified.

model_io.model: STZ-induced diabetic mouse and OIR mouse models

hypotheses.hypothesis: PTP4A3 overexpression promotes EC proliferation, migration, and permeability via PI3K-AKT activation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: PTP4A3 inhibition reversed EC dysfunction; PI3K/AKT inhibition partly alleviated effects

title: Isobaric quantitative proteomics reveals altered extracellular matrix, cytoskeletal, and degradation pathways in glaucomatous trabecular meshwork cells
task: Identify altered proteomic signatures in glaucomatous trabecular meshwork cells via isobaric quantitative proteomics
key_results: TMTpro-based quantitative proteomics identified 206 upregulated and 42 downregulated proteins in GTM vs NTM cells (FDR<0.1); dysregulated pathways: extracellular matrix (DCN, COL4A1, CHI3L1), Wnt signaling (FZD1/7, GSK3B), cytoskeletal regulation (ROCK2, MSN, TPM2, VIM, NF2), protein degradation; GTM nucleoli significantly larger, DIAPH2 more distended into cytosol
comments: Comprehensive proteomic profiling identifies candidate therapeutic targets but functional validation of dysregulated proteins in glaucoma pathogenesis requires follow-up studies.

model_io.model: Confluent trabecular meshwork cells (GTM vs NTM, n=5 each)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Glaucomatous TM cells exhibit dysregulation of extracellular matrix, cytoskeletal, and protein degradation pathways
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: multiple pathways significantly dysregulated in GTM

title: Degron models: a toolbox for rapid in vivo depletion of essential proteins regulating mRNA metabolism
task: Generate and characterize degron-tag models for rapid in vivo protein depletion of mRNA metabolism factors
key_results: Generated 7 degron-tag models (dTAG/FKBP or BromoTag) for Cnot1, Pan2, Tent5a/4b, Dcp2, Rnasel, Tsg101; dTAG shows dose-dependent degradation in cells and variable in vivo efficacy; BromoTag ineffective in vivo; CNOT1 depletion impairs cell division and poly(A) tail maintenance
comments: Comprehensive toolkit with important caveats about tag-position artifacts and route-dependent in vivo efficacy limiting universal applicability.

model_io.model: HEK293T and other mammalian cell lines (degron-tagged proteins)

hypotheses.hypothesis: dTAG and BromoTag systems enable rapid and reversible protein depletion in vivo
hypotheses.result: Partially confirmed: dTAG effective but variable by protein and route; BromoTag inactive in vivo

hypotheses.hypothesis: CNOT1 is essential for cell division and mRNA poly(A) tail maintenance
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: depletion impairs both processes

title: Glycine alleviates ovarian granulosa cell ferroptosis induced by ERα-mediated internalization of polystyrene microplastics
task: Determine glycine's mechanism in alleviating PS-induced ferroptosis in ovarian granulosa cells
key_results: Polystyrene microplastics (PS) induced ferroptosis in granulosa cells via ERα-mediated internalization; PS activated YAP1-ACSL4 signaling and lipid peroxidation; glycine alleviated ferroptosis by modulating lysosome-dependent ferritin degradation (ferritinophagy) via PAT1; restored iron homeostasis
comments: Links environmental contaminant exposure to ferroptosis through iron dysregulation; therapeutic intervention validated but molecular details of PAT1-ferritinophagy coupling need clarification.

hypotheses.hypothesis: PS internalization via ERα activates YAP1-ACSL4 signaling inducing ferroptosis
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: PS exposure increases ACSL4 and iron overload in granulosa cells

hypotheses.hypothesis: Glycine restores iron homeostasis via PAT1-dependent ferritinophagy
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: glycine treatment alleviates ferroptosis and restores iron balance

title: Lysosomal phosphoinositide turnover acts upstream of RagGTPase–mTORC1 and controls muscle growth
task: Elucidate role of lysosomal phosphoinositides in mTORC1 signaling and muscle growth
key_results: PI3P and PI(3,5)P2 regulate LAMTOR complex stability at lysosomes; MTM1-mediated phosphoinositide remodeling suppresses RagGTPase–mTORC1 during ER stress; restoring mTORC1 activity or phosphoinositide homeostasis rescues muscle growth in myopathy models
comments: Identifies a mechanistic intermediate between ER stress and metabolic checkpoint but in vivo phosphoinositide dynamics remain incompletely characterized.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Lysosomal phosphoinositides (PI3P, PI(3,5)P2) regulate LAMTOR complex dynamics and mTORC1 localization
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: MTM1-dependent remodeling alters complex stability

hypotheses.hypothesis: ER stress suppresses mTORC1 via MTM1-mediated phosphoinositide depletion
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: remodeling suppresses RagGTPase signaling during ER stress

title: Tracing the origins of molecular signals in food through integrative metabolomics and chemical databases
task: Tracing molecular signal origins in food through untargeted metabolomics and multi-database annotation
key_results: ~24,000 molecular features across 500 foods; revealed xenobiotic signatures and unexpected natural products; chemical patterns characteristic of food groups
comments: Large-scale food metabolomics resource, but unannotated signals remain poorly characterized; environmental attribution complex.

outputs: chemical annotations (pharmaceuticals, agrochemicals, contaminants, natural products)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Systematic food metabolomics links agricultural practices and environmental exposure to food chemistry
hypotheses.result: Revealed xenobiotic signatures, contaminant pathways, and unexpected natural products across food groups

title: Artificial intelligence-guided design of LNPs for in vivo targeted mRNA delivery via analysis of the spatial conformation of ionizable lipids
task: AI-guided ionizable lipid design for organ-targeted mRNA delivery via lipid spatial conformation analysis
key_results: ML-optimized lipid P1 (three-tail cone-shaped conformation) enabled spleen-targeted mRNA delivery; triggered strong antibody/T-cell responses; marked tumor suppression in preclinical models
comments: Excellent integration of MD simulations and ML for rational design, but translation to human studies and off-target effects at scale remain to be addressed.

inputs: ionizable lipid structures (varied amino heads, linkers, tails)

outputs: predicted delivery efficiency and organ targeting

hypotheses.hypothesis: Lipid spatial conformation (three-tail cone-shape) determines organ specificity and endosomal escape in mRNA delivery
hypotheses.result: P1-based LNP enabled spleen targeting and robust immune response; tumor suppression in preclinical models

title: A chromosome level reference genome for the pecan weevil, Curculio caryae
task: Chromosome-level reference genome assembly and annotation of pecan weevil
key_results: 2.2 Gb assembly (N50 132 Mb, BUSCO 95.4%); ~19,000 protein-coding genes; >80% repeat content
comments: High-quality reference is valuable for pest genomics, but functional annotation and comparative analysis await expansion.

title: Camouflaged object detection via context and texture-aware hierarchical interaction
task: Camouflaged object detection via context and texture-aware hierarchical feature interaction
key_results: CTHINet outperformed state-of-the-art on CAMO, COD10K, NC4K benchmarks; texture label supervision improved detection of imperceptible cues; strong performance on polyp segmentation downstream task

hypotheses.hypothesis: Hierarchical context-texture interaction and multi-scale feature aggregation enable robust camouflaged object detection

title: Canine gait analysis using inertial sensors and deep learning for orthopedic and neurological disorders
task: Deep learning-based gait analysis for canine orthopedic and neurological disorder classification
key_results: 0.96 multiclass accuracy (healthy/orthopedic/neurological); 0.85 binary (healthy/non-healthy) in cross-subject generalization on 29 dogs
comments: Small sample size (n=29) limits generalizability; sensor placement standardization and motion artifact handling are not addressed.

inputs: 3-axis accelerometer and gyroscope readings from wearable sensors

hypotheses.hypothesis: Inertial-based deep learning distinguishes neurological from orthopedic gait abnormalities with high accuracy
hypotheses.result: 0.96 multiclass accuracy; 0.85 binary accuracy with cross-subject generalization

title: Systematic multi-reference vertebrate ACE2 sequence similarity analysis predicts species susceptibility to SARS-related sarbecoviruses
task: Prediction of host species susceptibility to sarbecovirus infection via ACE2 sequence similarity
key_results: Analyzed 825 vertebrate ACE2 sequences; predictions correlated with in vitro infection experiments and literature; primates and ungulates ranked highest susceptibility
comments: Sequence similarity is a useful but imperfect predictor—functional binding kinetics and cell-type expression patterns also influence true infection risk.

hypotheses.hypothesis: ACE2 sequence similarity across species predicts SARS-CoV-2 infectivity
hypotheses.result: MrSARS predictions correlated with experimental infection and literature data

title: Modified Smith predictor-based fuzzy self-tuning FOPID controller for mean arterial pressure regulation
task: Adaptive fuzzy self-tuning FOPID control with modified Smith predictor for MAP regulation
key_results: FLST–FOPID–MSP architecture with 60 s delay compensation; faster rise/settling, reduced overshoot, lower integral error vs. PID and FOPID alone; clinically acceptable performance under inter-patient variability

hypotheses.result: Faster transient response, lower overshoot, reduced integral error vs. conventional PID; robustness to inter-patient variability and disturbances

title: InterFeat: a pipeline for finding interesting scientific features
task: Automated discovery and ranking of interesting features in biomedical data via ML, knowledge graphs, and LLMs
key_results: InterFeat surfaced risk factors years ahead of literature discovery (time-split eval); 21% of suggestions appeared post-publication; human evaluation: 28% overall interesting, 40–53% high-ranked interesting vs. 0–20% SHAP/L1 baselines
comments: Addresses important open problem of operationalizing scientific interestingness, but definition remains subjective; literature-based validation is inherently biased toward published findings.

model_io.model: InterFeat pipeline (ML + knowledge graphs + literature + LLM)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Integrating ML, knowledge graphs, literature, and LLM judgment identifies scientifically interesting features ahead of discovery
hypotheses.result: 21% of top suggestions appeared in literature after time-split; 40–53% high-ranked candidates deemed interesting by physicians

title: Fractal-fractional modeling of chronic myelogenous leukemia immune dynamics using Laguerre wavelets method
task: Fractal-fractional modeling of tumor-immune dynamics in chronic myelogenous leukemia
key_results: Novel Atangana–Baleanu fractal-fractional extension of Moore-Li model; theoretical foundations (existence, uniqueness, positivity, stability) established; Laguerre wavelets numerical solution with convergence validation

inputs: naive T-cells, effector T-cells, CML cell populations

outputs: solution trajectories under varying fractal/fractional orders

hypotheses.hypothesis: Fractal-fractional operators capture memory and geometric complexity in tumor-immune dynamics better than standard models

title: Stabilizing updates in differentially private stochastic gradient descent with buffered rejection
task: Stability improvement in differentially private SGD via buffered rejection and phased decay
key_results: 0.5–2% accuracy gains over standard DP-SGD and selective update baselines; membership inference AUC ≈0.5 (no privacy weakening)
comments: Practical improvements are modest and come at the cost of additional hyperparameters; applicability depends on computational budget and privacy requirements.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Buffered comparison and phased decay improve DP-SGD convergence under tight privacy budgets
hypotheses.result: 0.5–2% accuracy improvement vs. baselines without weakening empirical privacy

title: Allophycocyanin inhibits HIV-1 gp120 and reverse transcriptase through enthalpy-driven binding and antioxidative protection: integrative computational and experimental insights

key_results: Stable APC-gp120/RT/protease interactions (MD simulations); moderate enthalpy-driven gp120-APC binding (ITC); reduced viral protein and RT activity in cells; antioxidant protection

hypotheses.result: Stable gp120-APC binding; reduced RT activity and viral protein in cells; antioxidant protection

title: Ensuring the integrity of AI models: a blockchain-based approach for protecting medical imaging training data
task: Blockchain-based integrity protection of medical imaging datasets for AI model robustness
key_results: Hyperledger Fabric + Private IPFS framework prevents unauthorized access and tampering; cryptographic assurance of MRI image integrity; validated performance via Hyperledger Caliper

title: Injection locking of Rydberg dissipative time crystals

title: Unusual diffusion of chiral active Brownian particles in deformable and displaceable media

title: Collapse of Jahn-Teller phonons in La1−xSrxMnO3 with weak magnetoresistance
task: Phonon collapse and spin-phonon coupling in weakly magnetoresistive ferromagnetic manganites La₁₋ₓSrₓMnO₃
key_results: Jahn-Teller optical phonons collapse above Curie temperature despite weak CMR; quasielastic scattering increases while conventional magnon/phonon dispersions persist; giant EPC in charge/orbital channel identified
comments: Reveals that strong Jahn-Teller coupling drives magnetoresistance through carrier-trapping dynamics rather than magnetic order collapse.

title: Avoidance of rejuvenation: a stress test for evolutionary theories of aging

comments: The mathematical modeling is rigorous, but validation in natural populations beyond eusocial insects would strengthen the generality of the pathogen control hypothesis.
